---
title: "Fixed Point Iteration"
image: fixed-point.png
categories:
  - No derivatives
  - Fixed points
  - Linear convergence
  - Slow convergence
---

<!--
Copyright (c) 2026 Dhruv Azad. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Dhruv Azad, Apurva Nakade
-->


```{ojs}
//| output: false
// VM is the shared utility library loaded from js/make-function.js, js/make-derivative.js, js/padded-range.js, js/render-table.js.
VM = window.VM
```

Fixed point iteration is a way to solve an equation after rewriting it in the form

$$
x=g(x).
$$

Starting with an initial guess $x_0$, the method repeatedly applies $g$:

$$
x_{n+1}=g(x_n).
$$

If the sequence settles down to a value $\alpha$, then that value satisfies

$$
\alpha=g(\alpha).
$$

So $\alpha$ is called a fixed point of $g$.



```{ojs}
//| output: false
// setup computes the data shared by all three plots below: which rows are
// visible at the current step, the axis range for the cobweb diagram
// (square, since it plots y = x), and the sampled curve for g(x).
setup = {
  const visibleRows = result.rows.slice(0, Math.min(Number(stepControl), result.rows.length) + 1)

  // Axis ranges from ALL rows so the axes stay fixed while stepping.
  // The cobweb diagram uses a square plot (same range for x and y).
  const rangeValues = [Number(x0)]
  for (const row of result.rows) rangeValues.push(row.x, row.gx)
  const range = VM.paddedRange(rangeValues, {emptyRange: [-2, 2], relativePadding: 0.25, minPadding: 0.5})

  // A wider domain (double the padding of range) so the curve is already
  // sampled beyond the initial view — panning/zooming out reveals more of
  // the curve instead of blank canvas. The initial view stays range.
  const bufferRange = VM.paddedRange(rangeValues, {emptyRange: [-4, 4], relativePadding: 0.5, minPadding: 1.0})

  // Sample g across the buffer range for the smooth curve trace.
  const N  = 500
  const xs = Array.from({length: N}, (_, i) => bufferRange.lo + (bufferRange.hi - bufferRange.lo) * i / (N - 1))
  const ys = xs.map(x => result.g(x)).map(y => Number.isFinite(y) ? y : null)

  return {visibleRows, range, bufferRange, xs, ys, x0: Number(x0)}
}
```

```{ojs}
//| output: false
// mainPlot draws the cobweb diagram: g(x), the diagonal y = x, and the
// staircase path of iterates.
//
// _plotDiv is kept in a closure so Plotly reuses the same DOM node across
// reactive updates — this preserves zoom and pan when the step changes.
mainPlot = {
  const Plotly = window.Plotly
  let _plotDiv = null

  return ({visibleRows, range, bufferRange, xs, ys, x0}) => {
    const data = [
      // The iteration function g(x)
      {x: xs, y: ys, type: "scatter", mode: "lines", name: "g(x)", showlegend: true, line: {color: "#2563eb", width: 3}},
      // The diagonal y = x — fixed points lie where g(x) crosses this line.
      // Spans the buffer so it's still visible when the user pans/zooms out.
      {x: [bufferRange.lo, bufferRange.hi], y: [bufferRange.lo, bufferRange.hi], type: "scatter", mode: "lines", name: "y = x", showlegend: true, line: {color: "#111827", width: 2, dash: "dash"}},
      // The x-axis reference
      {x: [bufferRange.lo, bufferRange.hi], y: [0, 0], type: "scatter", mode: "lines", showlegend: false, line: {color: "#94a3b8", width: 1}}
    ]

    if (visibleRows.length > 0) {
      // Build the cobweb staircase path.
      // From x_i on the diagonal, go vertically to (x_i, g(x_i)) on the curve,
      // then horizontally to (g(x_i), g(x_i)) on the diagonal.
      const cobwebX = []
      const cobwebY = []
      if (Number.isFinite(x0)) { cobwebX.push(x0); cobwebY.push(x0) }
      for (const row of visibleRows) {
        if (!Number.isFinite(row.x) || !Number.isFinite(row.gx)) continue
        cobwebX.push(row.x,  row.gx)
        cobwebY.push(row.gx, row.gx)
      }

      data.push(
        // The staircase path
        {x: cobwebX, y: cobwebY, type: "scatter", mode: "lines+markers", name: "Cobweb path", showlegend: true,
          line: {color: "#dc2626", width: 2}, marker: {color: "#dc2626", size: 7}},
        // Green dots marking each iterate x_i on the diagonal
        {
          x: visibleRows.map(r => r.x).filter(Number.isFinite),
          y: visibleRows.map(r => r.x).filter(Number.isFinite),
          type: "scatter", mode: "markers+text", name: "Iterates", showlegend: true,
          text: visibleRows.filter(r => Number.isFinite(r.x)).map(r => `x_${r.i}`),
          textposition: "top center",
          marker: {color: "#16a34a", size: 9, symbol: "circle", line: {color: "white", width: 1}}
        }
      )
    }

    const layout = {
      margin: {l: 0, r: 0, t: 0, b: 0},
      xaxis: {title: "x", range: [range.lo, range.hi], zeroline: true},
      yaxis: {title: "y", range: [range.lo, range.hi], zeroline: true, zerolinecolor: "#94a3b8", gridcolor: "#e2e8f0", linecolor: "#cbd5e1"},
      legend: {orientation: "h", y: -0.18},
      hovermode: "closest",
      uirevision: "static",  // keeps zoom/pan state across reactive updates
      autosize: true
    }
    const config = {responsive: true, displaylogo: false}

    // First render: create the div. Later renders: update in place (preserves zoom).
    if (!_plotDiv) {
      _plotDiv = document.createElement("div")
      _plotDiv.className = "plotly-box-large"
      Plotly.newPlot(_plotDiv, data, layout, config)
      new ResizeObserver(() => Plotly.Plots.resize(_plotDiv)).observe(_plotDiv)
    } else {
      Plotly.react(_plotDiv, data, layout, config)
    }
    return _plotDiv
  }
}
```

<div class="ojs-controls-wide">

```{ojs}
viewof gText = {
  const el = Inputs.text({label: "Function g(x)", value: "cos(x)", placeholder: "Example: cos(x), sqrt(x + 2), exp(-x)"})
  el.style.width = "100%"
  return el
}
```

<div class="ojs-control-grid">

```{ojs}
viewof x0 = Inputs.number({
  label: "Initial guess x₀",
  value: 1,
  step: 0.1
})
```

</div>
</div>

<div class="ojs-step-row">

```{ojs}
viewof stepControl = {
  const el = Inputs.range([0, maxIterations - 1], {step: 1, value: 0, label: "Step"})
  el.style.width = "100%"
  return el
}
```

```{ojs}
viewof maxIterations = Inputs.number({value: 20, min: 1, step: 1, label: "Max"})
```

</div>

```{ojs}
//| output: false
// result: runs fixed-point iteration with the current inputs.
//   rows — array of { i, x, gx, error }, one entry per iteration
//   g    — parsed function, reused for curve sampling in renderOutput
result = {
  const math = window.math

  const g        = VM.makeFunction(math, gText)
  const fallback = x => NaN

  if (!g) return {rows: [], g: fallback}

  const rows  = []
  const start = Number(x0)

  if (!Number.isFinite(start)) return {rows: [], g}

  let x = start
  let i = 0
  while (i < maxIterations) {
    const gx = g(x)

    // Stop if g(x) blows up — the iteration has diverged.
    if (!Number.isFinite(gx)) {
      rows.push({i, x, gx, error: NaN})
      return {rows, g}
    }

    const error = Math.abs(gx - x)
    rows.push({i, x, gx, error})

    x = gx
    i++
  }
  return {rows, g}
}
```

<div class="ojs-output-wide">

```{ojs}
mainPlot(setup)
```

:::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Convergence plots"}

::: {layout-ncol=2}

```{ojs}
// iteratesPlot draws xₙ vs n for the entire run — it doesn't depend on the
// current step, so it's just plotted once per run.
iteratesPlot = Plot.plot({
  width: 340, height: 300, marginLeft: 46,
  x: {label: "n"},
  y: {label: "xₙ"},
  marks: [
    Plot.lineY(result.rows, {x: "i", y: d => Number.isFinite(d.x) ? d.x : null, stroke: "#2563eb"}),
    Plot.dot(result.rows, {x: "i", y: d => Number.isFinite(d.x) ? d.x : null, fill: "#2563eb", r: 4})
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
// convergencePlot draws log₁₀|xₙ − g(xₙ)| vs log₁₀(n) with a linear
// regression line, for the entire run — it doesn't depend on the current
// step, so it's just plotted once per run.
convergencePlot = {
  const logRows = result.rows
    .filter(r => r.i > 0 && Number.isFinite(r.error) && r.error > 0)
    .map(r => ({n: Math.log10(r.i), y: Math.log10(r.error)}))

  const pointsName = "log₁₀|xₙ − g(xₙ)|"
  const marks = [Plot.dot(logRows, {x: "n", y: "y", fill: () => pointsName, r: 4})]
  const colorDomain = [pointsName], colorRange = ["#dc2626"]

  if (logRows.length >= 2) {
    const m     = logRows.length
    const sx    = d3.sum(logRows, d => d.n)
    const sy    = d3.sum(logRows, d => d.y)
    const sxy   = d3.sum(logRows, d => d.n * d.y)
    const sxx   = d3.sum(logRows, d => d.n * d.n)
    const denom = m * sxx - sx * sx
    if (denom !== 0) {
      const slope     = (m * sxy - sx * sy) / denom
      const intercept = (sy - slope * sx) / m
      const [xlo, xhi] = [logRows[0].n, logRows[m - 1].n]
      const lineName   = `slope ≈ ${slope.toFixed(2)}`
      marks.push(Plot.line(
        [{n: xlo, y: slope * xlo + intercept}, {n: xhi, y: slope * xhi + intercept}],
        {x: "n", y: "y", stroke: () => lineName, strokeDasharray: "6,4"}
      ))
      colorDomain.push(lineName)
      colorRange.push("#9333ea")
    }
  }

  const chart = Plot.plot({
    width: 340, height: 300, marginLeft: 46,
    x: {label: "log₁₀(n)"},
    y: {label: "log₁₀|xₙ − g(xₙ)|"},
    color: {domain: colorDomain, range: colorRange},
    marks
  })
  const legend = Plot.legend({color: {domain: colorDomain, range: colorRange}})

  const container = document.createElement("div")
  container.append(chart, legend)
  return container
}
```

:::

::::

:::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Iteration table"}

```{ojs}
// Iteration table — shows the entire run, not just the current step.
iterationTable = result.rows.length === 0
  ? document.createElement("div")  // always return a Node so Quarto's OJS runtime
                                     // never latches this declaration cell as hidden
  : VM.renderTable({
      html,
      headers: ["n", "x_n", tex`g(x_n) = x_{n+1}`, tex`|x_{n+1} - x_n|`],
      csvHeaders: ["n", "x_n", "g(x_n) = x_{n+1}", "|x_{n+1} - x_n|"],
      filename: "fixed-point-iterations.csv",
      rows: result.rows.map(row => [
        row.i,
        Number.isFinite(row.x)     ? row.x.toPrecision(10)    : "NaN",
        Number.isFinite(row.gx)    ? row.gx.toPrecision(10)   : "NaN",
        Number.isFinite(row.error) ? row.error.toExponential(4) : "NaN"
      ])
    })
```

::::

</div>


## What the picture is showing

The plot shows two curves:

- the function $y=g(x)$,
- the line $y=x$.

The red path shows the iteration. From the current point, the method moves vertically to the curve $y=g(x)$, then horizontally to the line $y=x$. Repeating this creates the cobweb pattern.

This visualization is useful because it shows whether the iteration appears to be moving toward a fixed point, moving away from it, or oscillating.

A common condition that helps guarantee local convergence is

$$
|g'(x)|<1
$$

near the fixed point.
